events.description: MacArthur Fellow and National Humanities Medalist Rebecca Newberger Goldstein joins us to discuss her latest book,The Mattering Instinct: How Our Deepest Longing Drives Us and Divides Us.

events.title: US strategy in a turbulent world
Basel III and Bank Capital Rules: A Conversation with Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman
events.description: Join Vice Chair for Supervision Bowman at the Cato Institute as she details her comprehensive review and what is next for bank capital requirements and Basel III.

events.title: Cato Next Gen Reception
events.description: Let’s be honest: Health care in America is a mess. It’s confusing and expensive, and there are massive gaps—and, despite what many assume, it is far from a free market. How did we end up here? And more importantly, what can we do about it?

events.description: President Trump has launched a war with Iran, leaving the American public with more questions than answers.

events.title: Website navigation
events.description: There’s something for everyone. From award-winning theater and music, to poetry and exhibitions, experience the power of the arts with us.

events.title: Browse by:
events.description: Top of page

events.title: Family Day: Mapping a New Nation
The Two Georges: Parallel Lives in an Age of Revolution
events.description: Our understanding of George Washington and King George III of Britain is clouded by myths that have accumulated over centuries. This exhibition explores the lives and legacies of the two adversaries during America’s war for independence. The Library of Congress brings their papers together for the first time to reveal the real men behind the myths.

events.title: Treasures from the Stephen Sondheim Collection
events.description: In 2025, the Library of Congress acquired the papers of Stephen Sondheim, one of the most celebrated figures in twentieth-century musical theater. This small display in the Performing Arts Reading Room features highlights from the collection, including lyrics and music from his musicals "Into the Woods," "Sunday in the Park with George," "Merrily We Roll Along," "Sweeney Todd" and "A Little Night

events.title: Ojibwe Poet Jane Johnston Schoolcraft
events.description: In celebration of Women’s History Month in March and National Poetry Month in April, the Library of Congress presents a display of manuscript writings of Ojibwe poet and knowledge keeper Jane Johnston Schoolcraft (1800–1842) (Bamewawagezhikaquay) of Sault Ste. Marie, Michigan.

events.title: 2026 Innovator in Residence Informational Webinar
events.description: The Library of Congress is seeking its next Innovator in Residence! Launched in 2017, the program invites creative thinkers to connect the Library’s digital collections with the American public in transformative ways. The Library is looking for proposals that engage new audiences through discovery, insight, inspiration and creative reuse of its collections. Join staff to learn more about the oppor

events.title: Isata Kanneh-Mason
events.description: Award-winning British pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason presents a solo recital at the Library of Congress, showcasing her poetic insight, expressive depth, and imaginative artistry.

events.title: Orientation to Legal Research: U.S. Federal Statutes
events.description: This installment of the Orientation to Legal Research Series — a monthly program taught by law reference librarians that introduces participants to legal sources and research techniques — provides an overview of U.S. statutory and legislative research. The session will cover how to find and use the United States Code, the United States Statutes at Large, and federal bills and resolutions.

events.title: Live! At The Library
events.description: The Library’s Thomas Jefferson Building and all exhibitions will be open for extended hours on Thursdays. Visitors are invited to enjoy happy hour drinks and food available for purchase in the Great Hall and the Jefferson Building’s beautiful architecture while immersing themselves in the Library’s exhibits, collections and programs.

events.title: Live! At the Library: Who Knew? Trivia Night
events.description: As the U.S. approaches its 250th anniversary, gather your friends for a themed evening of trivia spotlighting the Veterans History Project. Test your knowledge of U.S. and military history inspired by the stories and collections that preserve the voices and experiences of America’s veterans.

events.title: Darby O'Gill and the Little People (1959)
events.description: Albert Sharpe and a young Sean Connery star in this richly imaginative Irish folktale filled with leprechauns and moral bargains. Directed by Robert Stevenson, the film is celebrated for its pioneering visual effects and its gentle reflections on pride, aging and legacy. Color, 93 min. (Film)

events.title: The Passion of Joan of Arc (1928)
events.description: Renée Jeanne Falconetti delivers one of cinema’s most revered performances in Carl Theodor Dreyer’s silent masterpiece, selected for the National Film Registry by the Library of Congress. Silent, with live musical accompaniment by Ben Model. Black & white, 81 min. (Digital)

events.title: Pagination
events.description: The Italian Cultural Institute of Washington invites you to explore a curated selection of books for young readers and to take part in our monthly series of gatherings “In biblioteca in italiano.” Kids between 4 and 10 are warmly invited to join us and Fiorenza Castelli for a fun journey through won

events.title: Events DC presents its annual Passport DC in May 2026!
events.description: Experience the world in one place by planning a trip to Washington, DC. Passport DC offers a month-long journey that showcases the city's thriving international diplomatic community and diverse culture. This event is celebrated annually in May during International Cultural Awareness month and offers
